definition: External or internal forcing moving the system toward an alternative regime
measurement_note: Nutrient loading climate pressure maintenance backlog trust erosion debt demand or disturbance frequency

definition: Degree to which change becomes self-reinforcing
measurement_note: Algal shading erosion feedback distrust loop panic cascade or degradation spiral
Rising variance can suggest weakening stabilizing feedback in some systems
Rising autocorrelation can indicate critical slowing down when properly interpreted

definition: Rate at which the system returns after disturbance
measurement_note: Slower recovery can indicate declining resilience

definition: Ability to learn respond reorganize and preserve function
measurement_note: Redundancy diversity trust slack monitoring public capacity governance flexibility

definition: Ecological institutional cultural or technical memory supporting recovery
measurement_note: Seed banks records local knowledge professional expertise refugia and after-action learning

definition: Ability to detect warning signals accurately and early
measurement_note: Data coverage timeliness signal quality local knowledge integration and actionability

definition: Extent to which unequal exposure and ignored warnings are visible and actionable
measurement_note: Disaggregated exposure repair delays complaints participation and community reporting
